topic_title: Decomposition — breaking a big problem into smaller solvable parts
verdict: PASS — All checks clear. Implementation section (cycle-1 hard fail) now carries [N] markers on every process step and every worked-example stage; citation density exceeds the 60% threshold across all three assessed sections. One residual soft finding (undefined 'user stories' in section 7) does not reach the hard-fail threshold.

citation_density.primary_resource: [1] OpenStax — cited at introduction (lines 30, 32, 34) and repeatedly throughout Core Concepts, Implementation, Real-World Patterns, Best Practices, and Key Takeaways

section_5_core_concepts.note: All sub-sections (5.1–5.6) carry [1], [2], and/or [3] markers on factual assertions. Density adequate. Unchanged from cycle 1.

section_6_implementation.note: Every step in the 6-step repeatable process and every stage of the worked library-website example carries at least one [N] marker. Citation density in this section substantially exceeds the 60% threshold. Hard fail resolved.

section_7_real_world_patterns.note: All five patterns carry citation markers: software dev [1][2], recipe [3], medical [2][3], emergency response [2], AI system design [1][3]. Density adequate. Unchanged from cycle 1.

sequence_drift.note: No forward-referenced concept is defined or taught. All adjacent downstream concepts appear only as bare names with explicit deferral pointers. Unchanged from cycle 1.

length_sanity.deviation_from_lower_bound: 50w (1.7%) below 3000w lower bound — within 30% tolerance; not a fail
length_sanity.note: Corpus is ~2950w. Inline [N] markers added in cycle 2 do not materially change word count. 1.7% below standard lower bound is within the 30% tolerance band. No change from cycle-1 assessment.

topic_scope.note: Corpus teaches decomposition and its task-tree vocabulary (root, sub-task, leaf, level, iterative refinement) only. No adjacent concept (abstraction, pattern recognition, algorithms, pseudocode, flowchart, recursion, divide-and-conquer) is explained or defined at depth. All appear as bare names with deferral pointers where mentioned. Unchanged from cycle 1.

findings.detail: 'user stories' used in Real-World Patterns (section 7, software development sub-section) without a plain-language gloss. Carried forward from cycle 1 — not addressed in this revision. Suggested fix: add a one-phrase gloss inline, e.g. 'user stories — short descriptions of what a user wants to accomplish'.

beginner_readability.terms_defined_on_first_use: pass — decomposition (5.1 definition box), task tree (5.3), root/sub-task/leaf/level (5.3 table), iterative (5.5 inline definition) all defined at first appearance
beginner_readability.jargon_dumps: none — no paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously
beginner_readability.plain_register: pass — short sentences, active voice, learner addressed as 'you' throughout; no hedging phrases detected

soft_findings.detail: 'user stories' used without gloss in section 7 (Real-World Patterns — software development). Carried forward from cycle 1. Surfaces at HITL #2/#3 for author awareness; does not block corpus advancement.
